notes.anchor: by the senate's decree someone had to be left in charge
notes.note: A senatus consultum required a departing governor to leave a deputy holding imperium when no successor had arrived; with the war looming Cicero had to choose between his low-regarded quaestor, the absent Coelius, and his reluctant brother Quintus.
L. Mescinius Rufus, Cicero's quaestor in Cilicia, whose accounts and conduct Cicero distrusted; leaving the province in a quaestor's hands was the legal default but one Cicero found least acceptable.

Cicero's daughter Tullia was being matched to a new husband (the engagement to Dolabella, settled in his absence); de condicione refers to the negotiation of the marriage-alliance, which he had entrusted to Terentia.

Cicero's official letter (litterae) to the Senate reporting his Pindenissus victory, on which he hoped a supplicatio (public thanksgiving) and eventual triumph would be voted; he feared Atticus's absence had let it be handled carelessly.
